nativeRange.habitat: Steep outer reef slopes, drop-offs, caves and rocky overhangs at depths of approximately 20–80 m

diet.notes: Genicanthus species are the only obligate zooplanktivores within the Pomacanthidae family. In the wild, has been documented feeding on zooplankton high above the substrate. In captivity, generally accepts enriched frozen mysis, finely shaved frozen shrimp, high-quality marine angelfish preparations, spirulina, and marine algae. Multiple small feedings per day are commonly recommended, particularly during acclimation.

summary: A planktivorous lyretail angelfish from the Western Pacific that is generally considered reef-safe and one of the more aquarium-friendly angelfishes. Exhibits dramatic sexual dimorphism — males and females look like different species.
careNotes:
Genicanthus melanospilos belongs to the only planktivorous genus within Pomacanthidae, which makes it generally considered safer around corals and invertebrates than most other angelfishes. Sources sometimes flag rare nipping on soft corals such as Xenia, so it is most commonly described as reef-safe with occasional caveats.

The species exhibits striking sexual dimorphism. Males have a pale bluish-white body with vertical reddish-black bars and a distinctive black spot on the breast, paired with a forked yellow caudal fin edged in blue. Females are yellow dorsally and bluish-white ventrally with a bluish caudal fin edged in dark blue. The two sexes are visually so different that they are sometimes mistaken for separate species in the trade.

In the wild, the species has been observed living in loose harems consisting of one dominant male and several females on outer reef slopes and drop-offs at depths of around 20–80 m. Only one male per aquarium is generally recommended; males have been known to be aggressive toward other males of the same genus. Can be kept as a mated pair or as a small group of females, and a juvenile male will often emerge from an established female group through protogynous sex change.

An active, open-water swimmer that tends to need ample horizontal swimming space and good current. Tank-size recommendations vary widely across sources (125 gal / 473 L from LiveAquaria and TankFacts, 700 L from ReefApp); 500 L is used here as a cautious midpoint that reflects the species' need for open swimming room while remaining a realistic home-aquarium size. Because the species comes from deep reef habitats, generally advised to provide dim or shaded acclimation lighting initially.

Lifespan has not been directly documented for this species in the sources reviewed, but the closely related Genicanthus semifasciatus has been reported to live 10–15 years in well-maintained aquariums and a similar range is generally assumed here.

Water parameter notes: temperature averaged across three sources (LiveAquaria 22.2–25.6°C, FishBase 24.5–28.5°C, TankFacts 24.4–26.7°C), yielding a consensus range of approximately 24–27°C. pH averaged from two sources (LiveAquaria 8.1–8.4, TankFacts 7.8–8.4) — minimum pulled to 8.0. Salinity range 1.021–1.025 SG (LiveAquaria 1.020–1.025, TankFacts 1.023–1.026). Adult size 18 cm TL per FishBase / Wikipedia / ReefApp; LiveAquaria states 9 in (~23 cm), which is likely overstated.
breedingNotes: Genicanthus melanospilos is a protogynous hermaphrodite — all individuals are born female and the dominant fish in a group can transition to male. In the wild, the species forms harems of one male and several females, and pelagic spawning over the reef has been documented for the genus. Captive spawning is occasionally reported but raising larvae is generally considered very difficult in home-aquarium conditions.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Pomacanthidae, Acanthuriformes), max length (18.0 cm TL), temperature range (24.5–28.5°C, mean 27.1°C), depth (20–45 m), native range (Western Pacific: Indonesia to Fiji, Ryukyu Islands south to Rowley Shoals and New Caledonia), and harem/planktivore behavior. Temperature range higher than LiveAquaria; averaged into the final range.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size (125 gal / 473 L), temperature (76–80°F / 24.4–26.7°C), pH (7.8–8.4), salinity (1.023–1.026 SG). pH minimum (7.8) pulled the averaged minimum to 8.0; salinity averaged with LiveAquaria to 1.021–1.025.

additional.name: ReefApp
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (18 cm / 7.1 in), tank size (700 L / 185 gal — substantially larger than LiveAquaria/TankFacts), protogynous hermaphroditism, harem structure, and reef-safe-with-caution rating (rare nipping on soft corals like Xenia). Used in tank-size triangulation; final 500 L is a cautious midpoint.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy, original description (Holacanthus melanospilos Bleeker, 1857), max length 18 cm TL, depth 20–80 m, sexual dimorphism, protogynous reproduction, and IUCN status (Least Concern). Used for taxonomic/biological background only — not for husbandry parameters.

fish.breedingNotes: Protogynous hermaphrodite that forms harems in the wild. Pelagic spawning has been documented for the genus; captive spawning is occasionally reported but larval rearing is generally considered very difficult in home aquariums.
